name: Vegetable-Stuffed Omelets/Ginger Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon pineapple juice or orange juice
2 tablespoons nuts (toasted walnuts or almonds, chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare vegetables: Cut asparagus into 3-inch lengths, carrots into 3-inch sticks, and green onions into 2-inch lengths. Shred radishes and slice cucumbers thin (optional ingredients).
2. Cook vegetables: In a large saucepan, cook asparagus, carrots, and green onions in a small amount of boiling, lightly salted water for 7-9 minutes until crisp-tender; drain well.
3. Prepare the sauce: Stir together sweet-sour sauce, pineapple juice or orange juice, and grated ginger root (or 1/8 teaspoon ground ginger).
4. Make the omelets:
a. Combine eggs and water in a small bowl. Beat until combined but not frothy.
b. Heat cooking oil in an 8-10 inch skillet with flared sides over medium heat until a drop of water sizzles.
c. Add half of the egg mixture to the skillet, tilting to coat all sides. Cook for 1-2 minutes or until set around the edges.
d. Using a spatula, lift the eggs and let uncooked portion flow underneath. Cover with plastic wrap.
e. Repeat with remaining egg mixture.
5. Assemble: Spread some sauce on each omelet. Arrange steamed vegetables on one quarter of each omelet, fanning them to the edge. Fold each omelet over the vegetables, then fold again. Top with additional sauce and sprinkle with nuts.
Note: The author's name and date are not included in the recipe instructions.
